source_text: ASHMORE, Jack Haliburton.—b. 1900; mil. serv., 1918–19 and 1940–41, 2nd lieut.; sub-inspr. of police, B. Guiana, 1920; dist. inspr., 1923; travelling mag. and warden, Nos. 3 and 4 mining dists., 1923–24; county inspr., 1927; local comdnt. C.I.D., Cyp., 1932; dep. comsnr. of police, 1935; comsnr., 1940; air raids comdtd., Cyp., 1940–44; comsnr., Cyp. dist., St. John Ambulance Bde. overseas.

BAILEY, HERBERT ARTHUR. —B. 1895; war serv., 1914-16; accts. clk., K.U. Rlys. and H., 1919; ag. asst. ch. acctnt., 1923; asst. acctnt., 1925; port acctnt., 1930; dep. ch. acctnt., 1931; asst. ch. acctnt., 1934; ag. ch. do., Nov., 1934–Aug., 1935.

BADCOCK, RICHARD DORIAN, M.C. —B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll.; mil. serv., 1914-19; major, R.A.; asst. ch. of staff, O.E.T.A. (S). 1919-20; sub-dist. gov., Palestine, 1920; sec. Near East Conf., Cairo, 1921; admve. offr., Safad, 1924; Nablus, 1926; Jaffa, 1928; dep. dir. dept. of imigrn., 1930; asst. dist. comsnr., S. dist., 1934.

BAILEY, MORRIS, O.B.E. (1937).—B. 1892; mily. serv., 1914–20; dep. mily. gov., O.E.T.A.(S), 1918; Jericho, 1918; Nablus, 1919; Hebron, 1919; asst. dist. gov., 1920; dist. offr., Gaza, 1922; do., Hebron, 1924; admstve. offr., Gaza, Hebron, Beersheba, 1927; Nablus, Jenin, Tulkarm, 1929; asst. dist. comsnr., N. dist., 1932–37; dist. comsnr., Haifa and Samaria dist., 1937.
BECKINGSALE, STANLEY, F.I.A.—B. 1899; govt. actuary, N.Z. govt., 1937.

BELGRAVE, WILLIAM NORMAN CUMMINS, B.A. (Cantab.), Camb. Univ. Dip. of Agr.—B. 1891; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; asst. mycologist, agri. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; plant physiologist, Jan., 1920; ch., divn. of soils and plant physiology, Feb., 1927; deleg. to internat. soc. of soil science, Konisberg, 1929; deleg., 2nd imp. mycological confce., London, 1929; asst. to dir., in addn., June, 1930; in ch., divn. of agr. chem., in addn., Sept., 1930; ch. resch. offr., Sept., 1931; dir., agr., S.S., and advers. on agr., M.S., 1939.
BEVIS, James Henry, B.Sc. (hons. bot.) (Lond.).—b. 1904; ed. London Univ.; asst. mstr., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, 1927 (dip. in educ.) (Lond.); senr. mstr., 1944.

BLAKE, GEORGE STANFIELD, B.Sc. Geol. Lond., A.R.S.M. Lond., M.I.M.M.—B. 1876; ed. R. Schl. of Mines, London; on teaching staff, 1896-97; Imp. Inst. (mineral survey of crown colts.), 1901; Newfoundland and Canada, 1909-15; munitions inspect., Eng., 1916-19; geol. staff, Imp. Coll. of Sci., 1920-22; geol. advr., Palestine govt., 1922.

BOTHAMLEY, CHARLES MILD MAY.—B. 1891; ed. Terrace Schl. and Wellington Boys' Coll., N.Z.; comtee. clk., N.Z. H. of R., 1917; legis. coun., 1919; 2nd clk. asst., legis. coun., 1924; dep. clk. of parlmts. and clk. asst., legis. coun., 1931; clk. of parlmts., clk., legis. coun., and examr., petitions for pte. bills, 1934.

BOUCAUD, Joseph Errol.—b. 1904 ; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin., solr., sup. ct. Trin. and Tob., and conveyancer ; prin. offr. and 2nd dep. registr. and marshal, Trin., 1933 ; dep. registr. and marshal, sup. ct., 1935 ; registr.-gen., 1936 ; asst. cr. solr., 1943 ; cr. solr., 1949.

BOTHAMLEY, CHARLES MILDMAY.—B. 1891; ed. Terrace Schl. and Wellington Boys' Coll., N.Z.; comtee. clk., N.Z. H. of R., 1917; legis. coun., 1919; 2nd clk. asst., legis. coun., 1924; dep. clk. of parlimts. and clk. asst., legis. coun., 1931; clk. of parlimts., clk., legis. coun., and examr., petitions for pte. bills, 1934.

BROWN, CAPT. ERIC GORDON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr.—B. 1890; ed. Burton-on-Trent Gram. Schl., Malvern Coll., and Birmingham Univ.; asst. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., Oct., 1919; dist. engnr., grade II., Oct., 1924; ditto, grade I., Oct., 1928; ag. divsnl. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., Feb., 1933.

CARBERRY, Sir J. E. D., Kt. Bach. (1956).—b. 1893 ; ed. McGill Univ. ; barrister-at-law ; mil. serv., 1915–19 ; clk., cts., J'ca, 1927 ; res. mag., 1932 ; puisne judge, 1946 ; senr. 1950 ; ch. just., 1954 ; pres. of water ct., 1931.

CAMERON, ALLEN DUNCAN. — B. 1874; ed. at Rathmines Schl., Dublin; cler. asst., inspr. of villages, and sec. to bd. of health, B. Guiana, 14th Apr., 1890; 6th cls. clk., inspr. of pris., Oct., 1893; 5th ditto, 9th Feb., 1896; govt. offr., dept. of mines, Bartika, 8th Dec., 1896; ag. navig. offr. 2nd Mar., 1897, to 8th Dec., 1898; ag. mag.'s clk., Bartika, 1897-98; transfd., with same appt., Koriabo station, 19th Oct., 1899; J.P., 1899; mag.'s clk., Corentyne jud. dist., 1st May, 1900; 3rd cls. clk., pol. dept., 1st Nov., 1901; lieut., B. Guiana militia, 21st Nov., 1901; capt., 4th Nov., 1902; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 7th May to 23rd Dec, 1903; A.D.C. to gov., Aug., 1902, to Sept., 1904; ag. govt. agt. and stip. mag., N.W. dist., 22nd Jan., 1904, to 17th Feb., 1905; ag. stip. mag., S. Essequibo jud. dist., 1st Nov., to 30th Dec., 1905; ag. asst. stip. mag., Berbice jud. dist., 1st Jan., to 27th Apr., 1906; and stip. mag., West Coast Demerara jud. dist., 17th Oct., 1906, to 29th Jan., 1907.

CELESTAIN, BEAUMONT WEBSTER.—B. 1896; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1933; prin. offr., legal dept., Trinidad, Dec., 1933; prin. offr., registr.-gen.'s dept., Jan., 1936; dep. registr. gen., May, 1937; mag., sub. registr. and dep. marshal, Tobago, 1938.

CLAIRMONTE, FREDERICK ARCHIBALD CONRAD. — B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Codring- on Coll., and Ontario Business Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1909; war serv., 1916-19; ch. lk., pol. mags. cts., dist. "A," 1927; ag. pol. nag., various dists., on several occasions, 1928- 31; comsnr., income tax, 1936.

CLARKSON, CHARLES FRANCOIS.—B. 1881; ed. Boys' Model Schl. and Boys' High Schl., Durban; admitted atty., Natal prov. divn., Feb., 1905; offr., Durban Light Infy., and adjt.; mem., prov. coun., 1915; mem., exec. coun., 1925; senator, 1930; min., posts and tels. and pub. wks., Mar., 1933.

CLARKSON, CHARLES FRANCIS.—B. 1885; ed. Boys' Model Schl. and Boys' High Sch., admitted atty., Natal prov. div., 1914; ben. offr., Durban Light Infy., and adjt., 1915; coun., 1915; mem., exec. coun., 1925; min., posts and tele. and pub. works, 1930; min., posts and tele. and pub. works, 1933.

COBON, HUGH HORACE, A.S. (Australasia), M.Q.I.S.—B. 1892; Aus. Imp. Fees., 1915-17; authd. survr., Queensland, 1920; survr., F.M.S. rlys., Nov., 1920; survr., Penang, Sept., 1921; do., Kedah, Feb., 1924; do., F.M.S., July, 1925; asst. supt., Johore, Feb., 1930; do., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ag. supt., Johore, June, 1934; ag. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Nov., 1937.
CONEY, JAMES SHARPE. —B. 1884; G.W.R. 1900-03; Cape govt. rlys., 1903-10; S. African rlys. & harbrs., 1910-27; supt. (admnstve.), K.U. Rlys. and H., 1927; ch. asst. to gen. man., 1929.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, ALEXANDER RAPHAEL.—B. 1899; ed. at St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, and Dominica Grammar Schl.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; admitted to bar, Leeward Is., 1924; practised at bar, 1924-35; ag. crown atty. and mag., dist. "F", and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, Oct. 1931 to Jan. 1932; ag. crown atty., Dominica, and mem. exec. and leg. couns., in 1933-4-5; visiting just. of prisons and J.P., 1934; crown atty., and mag., dist. "E", and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, Jan., 1935; comsnr., sup. ct., Leeward Is., June, 1935; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1936-Feb., 1937; ag. admstr., Dominica, Nov., 1937 and Mar., 1938; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, Apr., 1938.
CRUICKSHANK, ARTHUR EDWARD.—Ed. at Elgin; law apprentice in Scotland; superior grade, traffic dept., Bengal and N.W. rly., 9$\frac{1}{2}$ years; traffic man., Uganda rly., 9th Aug., 1897.

CUSACK, J. R., C.B.E. (1956).—b. 1902 ; ed. Aravon Sch., Denstone Coll. and Royal Sch., Armagh ; asst. audr., Nig., 1929 ; audr., Leeward Is., 1935 ; Maur., 1938 ; audr. (later dir., aud.), Trin., 1945 ; dir., aud., Ken., 1949 ; dir., aud., E.A.H.C. servs., in addn., 1952 ; contrlr. and audr.-gen., Ken., and audr.-gen., E.A.H.C. servs., 1955–57.

DANIEL, FRANCIS de FORREST.—B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll.; admitted solr., 1910; asst. acctnt., Lagos rly., July, 1912; transfd., cust. dept. May, 1913; asst. dist. offr., N. Provs., Nigeria, July, 1914; res., Mar., 1930; staff grade, 1937.

DE VERTEUIL, ERIC J., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H. (Lond.)—B. 1881; asst. med. inspr. of health, Trinidad, Oct., 1930; ag. dep. surg.-gen., May, 1933; senr. med. inspr. of health, Aug., 1935; M.O.H., N.W. dist., Jan., 1936.

DE VILLIERS, ISAAC PIERRE, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. S. African Coll.; atty. and notary, served S.W. Africa, in 1st Eastern Rifles, 1914-15; France and Palestine, in R.F.A., 1916-19; dep. comsnr. (headqrs.) S.A. pol., Feb., 1928; comsnr., S. African pol., Dec., 1928.
EDWARDS, J. B.—b. 1916; ed. Crypt Gram. Sch., Gloucester; Br. P.O., 1933–51; asst. inspr. of traffic (telecom.), E.A.P. & T. admin., 1951; asst. telecom. contrl., 1955; senr., 1960–62.
EDWARDS, R. E.—b. 1924; ed. Machynlleth County Sch.; mil. serv., 1945–48; Br. P.O., 1941–50; E.A.P. & T. admin., techn. gr. I, 1950; asst. engr. gr. II, 1955; gr. I, 1956; senr. asst. engr., 1960–64.

EDWARDS, George Elmore.— b. 1896 ; ed. Grenada Gram. Sch. and Harrison Coll., Barbados ; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1919 ; practised at bar, Grenada, St. V. and B. Guiana, 1919–36 ; registr., sup. ct., St. V., 1936 ; mag., 2nd dist., 1938 ; labour comsnr., 1938 ; ch. registr., sup. ct. of Wind. and Lee. Is. and registr., sup. ct., Grenada, 1941 ; comsnr. inc. tax, Grenada, 1944 ; chmn., labour advisory bd., Grenada, 1945.
ENGLISH, Patrick Thomas, M.B.E.—b. 1902; clk., Montserrat, 1921; Antigua, 1927; clk. and customs offr., G. & E. Is. Col., 1931; author of Man's March through the Ages .

EPSTEIN, ALEXANDER, M.B.E. (1937). — B. 1886; ed. Hebrew Inst., Liverpool; Jews Hosp., W. Norwood; inspr., dept. of commerce and industry, Palestine, 1920; dist. offr., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1923; admstve. offr., S. dist., 1925.

FAWCETT, CAPT. ARTHUR EDWARD, B.A., B.A.I., (Dublin), A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr., —B. 1890; war. serv., 1916-19, R.E., France, Persia, Afghanistan, ment. in desp.; asst. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Apr., 1920; dist. engnr., Apr., 1925; do., grade I, Apr., 1929.

FELL, W. M., C.B.E. (1965).—b. 1916; ed. Newport High Sch.; mil. serv., 1940–46, capt.; asst. audr., Nig., 1946; audr., G. & E.Is. Col., 1950; senr. audr., Trin., 1954; redesig., dep. dir., audit, 1958; dep. dir., audit, S'pore, 1959–62; ret., re-aptd. dir. audit, S'pore, 1963.

FERGUSON, John Taylor, F.A.C.C.A.—b. 1900; ed. Barrhead High Sch. and Sch. of Accountancy; clk., K.U.R. & H., 1924; senr. clk., 1930; asst. acctnt., 1935; acctng. offr. (Port), 1940; rev. acctnt., 1942; asst. ch. acctnt., E.A.R. & H., 1950; secy., Ken. war bonus schemes comtee., 1944; mem., cotton policy comtee., Nyanza, 1948.

FARR, FRANK JAMES, M.B., Ch.B., L.D.S., D.M.R. & E.—B. 1900; radiologist, med. dept., Hong Kong, 1928. adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apr., 1892 to Apr., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apr., 1897 to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.-W. Frontier, India), 1888, asst. prov. marshal (ment. in deep., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899 to June, 1900; dist. comsnr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in deep., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps); substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteers; left the service, 1906; A.A.G., Northern com., India, 1906-7; chief constable, metrop. police, 1907-12; comdt.-gen., Rhodesian forces, 1912; ret. 1st Apr., 1923.

FERGUSSON, W. K.—b. 1910 ; ed. Harrison Coll., Barb., and Camb. Univ. ; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn ; clk. to atty.-gen., Barb., 1935 ; leg. dtfsmn., 1937 ; police mag., and coroner, dist. A, 1938 ; judge, Bridgetown petty debt. ct., 1944 ; mag., Trin., 1945 ; asst. to atty.-gen., 1952 ; leg. dtfsmn., 1954 ; joint author of the 1944 revised edn. of the Statute Laws of Barbados .
FORBES, PAUL HENRY.—B. 1892; admiralty (war staff), Aug.-Oct., 1914; B.E.F., 1914-19; asst. ch. acct., Palestine rlys., Aug., 1921; ch. acct., Aug., 1923; ch. acct., F.M.S. rlys., Dec., 1926.

FORBES, ALEX. WILL. WENTWORTH—Served 10 years in Royal Navy; served through Gaika and Gcaleka wars, 1878; assistant inspector Gold Coast constabulary, Aug., 1879; has been district commissioner of Palma and Leckie, 1880, Badagry, 1881, Lagos, 1882, and Cape Coast, 1884-5-6; on special service up the Niger in 1882; has also acted as adjutant and inspector of Artillery in the constabulary; inspector, 1888; retired, 1889.

FOX-STRANGWAYS, V., C.B.E. (1953).—b. 1898 ; ed. Winchester ; mil. serv., 1916–22, capt., and 1940–45, col. ; cadet, Nig., 1923 ; attd. C.O., 1929 ; dist. offr., 1931 ; Nyasa., 1933 ; res. comsnr., G. and E. Is. Col., 1941 ; attd. to C.O., 1946 ; under-sec., Pal., 1946 ; sec. for African affrs., Nyasa., 1948 ; liaison offr., Rhod.-Nyasa. royal comsn., 1938.

GEORGE, HENRY BERNARD.—B. 1880; clk., Imp. P.O., 1897; clk., G.P.O., Cape, 1900; clk., P.W.D., 1903; clk., C. and A.G.'s office, 1905; clk., audit office, Cape adminst., 1912; served, Great War, France, with S.A.O.E.F. and R.A.F.; offr. in ch., local govt. audit, 1920; prov. audr., Natal Prov., 1932; prov. audr., Transvaal Prov., 1933; author of "Standard Electricity Accounts in S.A."

GEORGE, HENRY BERNARD.—B. 1880; clk., Imp. P.O., 1897; clk., G.P.O., Cape, 1900; clk., P.W.D., 1903; clk., O. and A.G.'s office, 1905; clk., audit office, Cape admnstn., 1912; served, Great War, France, with S.A.O.E.F. and R.A.F.; offr. in ch., local govt. audit, 1920; prov. audr., Natal Prov., 1932; prov. audr., Transvaal Prov., 1933; author of "Standard Electricity Accounts in S.A."

GITTINS, JOHN WANSBROUGH, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Blundell's and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, Gold Coast, 1928; resigned (ill-health), 1929; cadet, Fiji, 1930; passed cadet, 1932; dist. comnr., 1933; clk, to couns., 1933; asst. sec., seort., 1937; re-designated admstve. offr. gde. II, 1938; dist. comsnr., N. dist. 1939.

GROSS, REGINALD DAVID, M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Cert. L.S.T.M. (with dist.).—B. 1899; ed. S. Afr. Coll. and Guy's Hosp.; med. offr., Malaya, Mar., 1927; ag. prof. of biology, Coll. of Med., Jan. to Sept., 1929; demonstr. in pub. health, Coll. of Med., Sept., 1929; ag. sr. health offr., Johore, Mar., 1930; ag. dep. S.M. & H.O., Perak, Feb., 1937; ag. sr. H.O., Kedah, Nov., 1937.

HALL, CAPT., ALFRED JOHN, M.I.C.E. chartered civ. engnr.—B. 1884; engnr. asst., N.E. rly. G. Britain, 1907-1912; asst. engnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rlys., Mar., 1912; section engnr., Apr., 1914; army serv., R.E., Aug., 1915-19; dist. engnr., grade II., F.M.S. rlys., Feb., 1920; grade I., Feb., 1924; dist. engnr. I., Ipoh dist., Mar., 1930.
HARTIGAN, THOMAS JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1936). —B. 1877; ch. acct., N.S.W. rlys., 1921-27; comptr., accs. and audit, 1928-31; transport commr. (finance), 1932; commr. for rlys., 1933.

HARNEY, EGBERT EWART.—B. 1898; clk. to registr.-gen., Antigua, 1916; 3rd audit clk., 1921; 2nd do., 1922; ch. clk., registr.'s office, 1927; comsnr. for oaths, 1927; ag. registr. and prov.-marshal, Dominica, 1931; prin., treasury dept., Mar., 1933; passed bar final exams. (Inner Temple) Dec., 1934; ag. mag., dist. F., May, 1935 to Feb., 1936; ag. treas., Mar.-Oct., 1936; temp. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Mar.-Oct., 1936; ch. registr., Leeward Is. and registr. and provost marshal, Antigua, 1936; treas. and compr., cust., Dominica, Dec., 1936; mem., exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, Jan., 1937; called to bar, Inner Temple, June, 1938.
HASKINS, HAROLD STANLEY, M.C., A.M.Inst. C.E. —B. 1887; ed. Bristol Univ.; sect. engnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rlys., May, 1919; divnl. engnr., July, 1929; dist. engnr., Aug., 1935.

HAYWARD, William Frederick, O.B.E. (1950).—b. 1891; ed. Suva Boys' Gram. Sch.; clk., G.P.O., Fiji, 1905; postal supt., 1927; acctnt., 1928; P.M.G., 1936; sec., comsns. of currency, mem., Suva Town Bd., mem., C.S. Bd.

HIBBERD, ERNEST. — B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Wimborne, Dorset; asst. loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., July, 1919; dist. loco. supt., Grade II, K. Lumpur, Mar., 1926; do. grade I, July, 1928; sr. running asst., Aug., 1932; ag. asst. ch. mech. engnr., cent. wrkshp., July, 1936.

HOARE, HENRY SAMUEL MALORTIE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1928; attd., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1928; attd., Anuradhapura kach., April, 1928; office asst., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1929; pvte. sec. to gov., Feb., 1931; landing survr., cust., Nov., 1932; ag. dep. collr., cust., July-Sept., 1933; temp. attd., C.O., Aug., 1936-Apr., 1937; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, June, 1937; sec. to finance sec., Apr., 1938.

HODGE, J. E., C.M.G. (1962), C.V.O. (1956), Q.P.M. (1955), C.P.M. (1953).—b. 1911; ed. Taunton Sch.; sub-inspr., J'ca constab., 1931; asst. supt. police, Nig., 1935; senr. asst. supt., 1944; senr. supt., 1949; dep. comsnr., 1953; comsnr., 1955; dep. I-G., 1960. (Nig. Govt. service.)

HOWARD, ARTHUR ERNEST SAMUEL.—B. 1891; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; sub-inspr., constab., Fiji, 1914; dist. comsnr., 1924; asst. sec. for Indian affrs., 1932; seconded as agt. and consul, Tonga, 1933; dist. comsnr., Lautoka, 1937; re-designated admstve. offr., gde. II, 1938; dist. comsnr., W. dist., 1938-39.

HUBBARD, PERCIVAL CYRIL.—B. 1902; ed. Mill Hill and Caius Coll., Cantab. (mod. languages scholarship); B.A. hons., 1923; LL.B. hons., 1924; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1925; cadet, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., May, 1928; ag. asst. dist. offr., Malaita, Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Ysabel, Jan., 1929; ch. mag. and legal advr., Br. Sol. Is. Prot., Feb., 1929; ag. Br. judge, joint ct. and judl. comsnr. for New Hebrides, 1930-32; ch. mag., Palestine, 1934.

HUBBARD, P. C.—b. 1902 ; ed. Mill Hill and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb. ; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple ; cadet, B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1928 ; ch. mag. and legal advr., 1930 and 1932–34; Br. judge of jt. ct., N. Heb. comsnr., 1930–32; ch. mag., Pal., 1934; relieving pres., 1941; pres., 1945; puisne judge, 1947; Nig., 1948; judge, Fed. of Nig., 1956.

HOWES, Richard John Clyde, M.B.E. (civ.).—b. 1906; ed. King Edward VII Sch., Lytham, Jesus Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (hons.); on mil. serv. 1941–42, hon. capt., K.A.R.; cadet, Ken., 1929; dist offr., 1931; p.s. to various govrs. and act. govrs. during 1933, 1937–40 and 1945–46; asst. sec., E.A. gov. conf., 1942; asst. ch. sec. II, E.A. gov. conf., and sec., H. comsnr., K.U.R. & H., 1944–45; sec. to D.A.R.A., 1945–46; accomps. E.A. del. to S.A. air transport conf., Cape Town, Mar., 1945.

HUNT, CAPTAIN JOHN CALVIN, J.P.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Aliwal, north division, Cape Colony, January, 1879; entered Austrian army, 1844; joined 5th cuirassier regiment; served at siege of Vienna, 1848, and campaign in Hungary in that year and 1849; engaged in all the principal actions till surrender of main Hungarian army to the Russian allies at Villagos; captain in 1852; with the army of observation on the Vistula, in Poland and Bukovina, in 1854 and 1855; received extra certificate from Emperor of Austria, for good service and bravery before the enemy; same year, 1855, obtained permission to proceed to England, where he joined 1st regiment Light Dragoons, British German Legion, at Shorncliff, for the Crimean war, at conclusion of which he volunteered for service, and embarked on board H.M.S. "Vulcan" for the Cape, Nov. 1856, in command of dragoons; on arrival in British Kaffraria, volunteered for active service for three years, and placed on full pay for that period; was disbanded again at the Cape in 1860, and immediately appointed acting deputy-assistant commissary-general, at the port of East London and outstations in British Kaffraria; Sept. 1861, was appointed special magistrate of the Gonulin district; served there till Feb., 1870, when he was appointed civil commissioner and resident magistrate of the Fort Peddie district, where he was actively employed in raising, organising, and arming upwards of 2,000 Fingoés and also European volunteers for service in the Gaika and Galika Kaffir Rebellion, 1877 and 1878.

JEFF, RICHARD HEIDLEY ALLISON.—B. 1889; jr. asst. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1913; dist. engnr., grade III, head office, July, 1915; supernum. dist. engnr., July, 1919; on sp. duty in S. India, Aug., 1919; dist. engnr., grade II, K. Lumpur, Jan., 1920; dist. engnr., grade I, Jan., 1924; ag. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1928-Aug., 1929; sr. dist. engnr., head office, June, 1929; ditto, Johore Bahru, Feb., 1931; divnl. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., Aug., 1932.

JEFF, RICHARD HEDLEY ALLISON.—B. 1889; jr. asst. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1913; dist. engnr., grade III, head office, July, 1915; supermny. dist. engr., July, 1919; on sp. duty in S. India, Aug., 1919; dist. engr., grade II, K. Lumpur, Jan., 1920; dist. engr., grade I, Jan., 1924; sr. dist. engr., head office, June, 1929; ditto, Johore Bahru, Feb., 1931; divnl. engr. F.M.S. rlys., Aug., 1932; ag. maint. and new wks. engr., Apr.-Dec., 1936; sec., Mui-Tsai Comsn., Hong Kong and Malaya, 1936; ag. asst. sec. for Ch. affrs., Oct., 1937.

KERR, A. E.—b. 1912; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., and Lond. Univ.; asst. govt. chem., Trin., 1941; dep., 1950. KE Hert admin sec't, coun KE Sch., mil. 1930 regis dev., Cairo 1946 poss in ag KE Cast mil. asst. 1943 asst. perm KE Nie asst eng con ch. KE Cli I.C offr agr I b. Ug Tr dir dir Gr 19 as re C S 19 s C a l d

flagged_value.position: KE Cli I.C offr agr I b. Ug Tr dir dir Gr 19 as re C S 19 s C a l d

source_text: LAURIE, William, D.S.O., M.D. (Glasgow), dip. (Tb.) (Wales).—b. 1909; ed. Glasgow Univ.; on mil. serv., 1939–45, col.; dir. filariasis res. unit, E.A.H.C., 1949; author of Blackwater Fever in Waziristan , etc.

LEE, J. W.—b. 1919; ed. Junr. Tech. Sch. and Tech. Coll., Horwich; asst. loco. supt., Pal. rlys., 1943; asst. mech. engr., E.A.R. and H., 1948; dist. mech. engr., 1954. LEE Kuo Tai.—b. 1921; ed. West China Union Univ.; asst. dental surg., H.K., 1949; dental surg., 1956.

McGEAGH, WILLIAM RYDER, M.A.—B. 1901; ed. Winchester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; dist. offr., Kenya, 1927; ag. asst. sec., secretariat, Apr., 1931 to June, 1932; admstve. offr., Zanzibar, 1934; asst. sec., secty., 1936; pvte. sec. to Sultan, 1937; asst. dist. comsnr., Palestine, Dec., 1937.

MARTINDALE, MILES HAY, Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1893; asst. dist. offr., 1919; pvte. sec. to gov., 1920; asst. sec., S.P. secret., 1923; ag. dist. offr., 1924-27; dist. offr., 1928; ag. crown coun. and ag. ch. regisr., 1933; asst. judge, high ct., Prot., 1934; judge, Prot. ct., Apl., 1937.

MAUDE, Henry Evans, O.B.E. (1949), M.B.E. (civ.).—b. 1906 ; ed. Highgate Sch. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge, M.A. (Cantab.) ; cadet, G. and E. Is. Col., 1929 ; dep. comsnr., W. Pac., 1931 ; admin. offr., 1932 ; native lands comsnr., G. and E. Is. Col., 1934 ; asst. dist. offr., Zanz., 1936 ; admin. offr. and native lands comsnr., G. and E. Is. Col., 1937 ; seconded for spl. duty in Pitcairn Is., 1940 ; ch. lands comsnr., G. and E. Is. Col., 1941 ; seconded to Br. Agency and Consulate, Tonga, 1941 ; seconded, Kingdom of Tonga, 1941 ; seconded to W. Pac. H.C., 1942 ; on spl. duty at C.O. and Br. Emb., Washington, 1946 ; res. comsnr., G. and E. Is. Col., 1946 ; seconded S. Pac. comsn. as dep. sec. gen., 1948 ; rep. G. and E. Is. Col. at confce. and seminar on educ. in Pacific countries, Honolulu, 1936 ; organ. colonization of Phoenix Is., 1937–39 ; reorgan. const. and legal code for Pitcairn Is., 1940 ; compiled report on reorgan. of pub. serv., Kingdom of Tonga, 1941 ; author of various papers on anthrop. of Gilbert Is.
MORRIS, JAMES.—Worked for twelve years as roller in Mr. Cooper's foundry, at Birmingham; appointed Feb. 1865 foreman of the rolling mill in the mint of Hong Kong.

MULLER, W. A., C.M.G. (1946), K.P.M. (1944), C.P.M. (1950).—b. 1898 ; ed. Univ. Coll. Sch. and Royal Coll. of Science ; mil. serv., 1915–19, lieut. ag. maj., 1939–40, col. ; o/c troops, Trin. ; asst. supt., police, Ceylon, 1920 ; supt., 1932 ; comsnr., police and comdnt., local forces, Trin. 1938 ; dir., civil aviation, 1940–42 ; comsnr., police, Tang., 1948 ; I.-G. of col. police, 1951.
NEISH, Donald Gordon.—b. 1905 ; ed. Bedford Sch. ; sub-inspr., J'ca. constab., 1933 ; 3rd cl. police inspr., 1936 ; 2nd cl., 1940 ; 1st cl., 1943 ; supt., police, Trin., 1949.

NEILSON, MAJOR JOHN BARRIE., M.C. and 2 Bars, M.A. (Oxon.), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1896; asst. inspr., schls., Perak, July, 1921; asst. mast., govt. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Aug., 1921; sr. asst. mast., Raffles Inst., Apr., 1926; inspr., schls., N. Sembilan, Sept., 1928; do., Pahang, Dec., 1930 and Jan., 1935; snr. educn. offr., B. Malaya, Nov., 1936; prin., King Edward VII schl., Taiping, Jan., 1938.

NURSE, SYDNEY HARRINGTON—B. 1889; ed. Harrison Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1905; clk., pol. mag's ct., dist. "D," 1919; ag. pol. mag., various dists. on several occasions 1922-36; pol. mag., dist. "E," July, 1936.

PALMOUR, FREDERICK JOHN CLIFFORD, P.A.S.I.—B. 1884; ed. Daventry Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxon; survr., Can. Pac. Rly., 1911; valuer, valn. dept., I.R., 1913; adjt., R.E., ord. surv. office, Southampton, 1918; land agent, lands branch, W.O., 1920; ex. offr., land dept., Nigeria, 1928; land offr. and survr., Gambia, 1934.

PARSONS, T. R., I.S.O. (1956), M.B.E.—b. 1898 ; mil. serv., 1916–19 ; boy clk., G.P.O., 1914 ; asst. clk. (clerical offr.), 1916 ; trans. to C.O., 1923 ; higher clerical offr., 1938 ; higher exec. offr., 1944 ; senr. exec. offr., 1948 ; chief exec. offr., 1954.
PHILP, DAVID.—B. 1893; asst. acct., F.M.S. rlys., Dec., 1919; ag. constrn. acct., Mar., 1921; ag. dep. acct., Oct., 1930 and Apr., 1932; ag. asst. sec. to gen. man., June, 1931.

PHILP, DAVID.—B 1893; asst. acct., F.M.S. rlys., Dec., 1919; ag. dep. acct., in 1930, 1932, 1934 and 1935; ag. asst. sec. to gen. man., June, 1931; expend. acct., Aug., 1935; ag. ch. acct., Apr., 1938.

PITBLADO, DAVID BRUCE, M.A.—B. 1912; ed. Strand Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (schol. 1930-34); hist. tripos part 1 and law tripos part 2; B.A., 1933; hist. tripos part 2. cls. 1, 1934; supervisor in hist., Emm. Coll., 1934-35; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1935; asst. pvte. sec. to S. of S. for Dom. Affrs. (Mr. Malcolm Macdonald) 1st July, 1937; ag. prin., 1st June, 1939.

PORTER, G. H. A.—Appointed colonial secretary of the Virgin Islands, 9th July 1844; registrar of deeds, August, 1846; notary public, 1851; registrar in Admiralty, March, 1847; clerk to court of Petty Sessions, February, 186; private secretary to Presidents Price, Longden, and Sir A. Rumbold, Bart., from 1859 to 1865; member of executive council, 1849 to 1852, when he resigned, but was re-appointed, August, 1865; is major of the Virgin Islands artillery.

flagged_value.position: clerk to court of Petty Sessions

source_text: PRETHEROE, EDWARD OWEN, M.C., B.A.; LL.B.—B. 1896; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; dmstve. offr., 1922; asst. commr., lands, 1924, g. commr., lands, 1924; asst. sec., S. Provs., 1924; pol. mag., 1928; ag. ch. registr., sup. ct. on is; crown counl., 1930; ag. solr.-various occs occasions. gen. on vaJS, Jan Lodewyk.—B. 1878; ed. at PRET Bloemfontein; joined O.F.S. repub. Grey, Mar., 1887; asst. mag., Edenburg, civ. Vrede, 1904; Lindley, 1908; det. asst. 19, Dewetsdorp, Feb., 1910 to Oct., 1913; Litz, Nov., 1913; mag., Reitz, 1918; Hoopstad, Feb., 1919; Boshof, Oct., 1919; Frankfort, Oct., 1923; Senekal, June, 1929; Vereeniging, Feb., 1930; Lichtenburg, Mar., 1933; Port Elizabeth, Feb., 1934; Pretoria, Mar., 1936.

PRENTICE, ROBERT JOHNSTON CALDER, F.S.I., M.R.San.I., M.C.Q.S.—B. 1893; P.W.D., 1909; asst., ch. quantity survr., Union of S. Africa, 1933; ch. quantity survr., 1935; pres.-in-ch., Inst. S.A. Architects and Chapter of Quantity Surveyors, 1938; pres., Chapter S.A.Q.S., 1934-35, 1935-36 and 1939-40.
REDFEARN, PERCY WILLIAM.—B. 1890; loco. acctnt., F.M.S. rlys., Dec., 1919; asst. acctnt., Jan., 1925; fincl. offr., July, 1928; offctg. sr. asst. acctnt., F.M.S. rlys., Aug., 1933.

RICHARDSON, LAWRENCE.—B. 1890; stock verifier, F.M.S. rlys., Apr., 1920; stores acct., Oct., 1920; asst. acct., Jan., 1925; finan. offr., stores dept., July, 1928; asst. stores supt., Oct., 1935; ag. stores supt., Jan., 1937.

ROCK, ALFRED MILTON. — B. 1888; ag. 3rd outdoor offr. and harbmr., treasury, Dominica, 1913-15; clk. and interpreter to mag., dist. "E." Mar., 1915; clk., mag., registr. and provost marshal "A," comsnr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Montserrat, Sept., 1915; clk. to mag., dist. "A" and "B," dep. coroner, Antigua, Nov., 1920; senr. clk., registr.'s office, clk., sup. ct. law libry., "A" comsnr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Dominica, May, 1927; prin. for duty as registr. and provost marshal, registr. of titles, admr. of estates and registr.-gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Dominica, Apr., 1929.
